@@ -545,7 +545,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-24hd","title":"race\! macro: first completion with loser cancellation","description":"# race\! Macro Implementation

## Goal

Implement the race\! macro for returning the first future to complete while properly cancelling and draining losers.

## Semantics

```rust
// Basic usage - returns Either-like enum
let winner: Race3<A, B, C> = race\!(fut_a, fut_b, fut_c).await;

match winner {
    Race3::First(a) => println\!('A won: {:?}', a),
    Race3::Second(b) => println\!('B won: {:?}', b),
    Race3::Third(c) => println\!('C won: {:?}', c),
}
```

## Key Properties

### 1. First Wins
First future to return Poll::Ready is the winner.

### 2. Losers Cancelled
All non-winning futures are cancelled:
- Cancel request sent
- Drain phase (bounded by budget)
- Finalize phase
- Obligations resolved

**This is the CRITICAL difference from other race implementations.** Losers are not simply dropped - they go through the full cancellation protocol.

### 3. Wait for Drain
race\! does NOT return until all losers have completed cancellation:
```rust
// Pseudocode
loop {
    // Poll all pending
    for i in 0..futures.len() {
        if \!completed[i] {
            match futures[i].poll(cx) {
                Poll::Ready(outcome) => {
                    if winner.is_none() {
                        winner = Some((i, outcome));
                        // Cancel all others
                        for j in 0..futures.len() {
                            if j \!= i && \!completed[j] {
                                futures[j].cancel(CancelReason::RaceLost);
                            }
                        }
                    }
                    completed[i] = true;
                }
                Poll::Pending => {}
            }
        }
    }
    
    if completed.iter().all(|&c| c) {
        break; // All done, including losers
    }
}
```

### 4. Loser Outcomes
Losers complete with Outcome::Cancelled(RaceLost). Their outcomes are available for inspection:
```rust
let result = race\!(fut_a, fut_b).await;
assert\!(result.loser_outcomes().all(|o| o.is_cancelled()));
```

### 5. Cancellation Propagation
If parent region is cancelled:
- ALL futures cancelled (including current 'winner')
- race returns Cancelled outcome

## Implementation

### Race Enum
```rust
pub enum Race2<A, B> {
    First(A),
    Second(B),
}

pub enum Race3<A, B, C> {
    First(A),
    Second(B),
    Third(C),
}
// ... up to Race16
```

### Race Future
```rust
pub struct RaceFuture2<F1, F2> {
    fut1: MaybeCancelling<F1>,
    fut2: MaybeCancelling<F2>,
    winner: Option<usize>,
}

enum MaybeCancelling<F: Future> {
    Running(F),
    Cancelling(F),
    Done(Outcome<F::Output, Error>),
}

impl<F1, F2> Future for RaceFuture2<F1, F2>
where
    F1: Future + Cancel,
    F2: Future + Cancel,
{
    type Output = Race2<F1::Output, F2::Output>;
    
    fn poll(self: Pin<&mut Self>, cx: &mut Context<'_>) -> Poll<Self::Output> {
        // 1. Poll all non-done futures
        // 2. If any completes and no winner yet, mark winner and cancel others
        // 3. When ALL done (including cancelled losers), return winner
    }
}
```

### Cancel Trait
Futures in race\! must implement Cancel:
```rust
pub trait Cancel: Future {
    /// Initiate cancellation
    fn cancel(&mut self, reason: CancelReason);
    
    /// Check if cancelled
    fn is_cancelled(&self) -> bool;
}
```

## Macro Syntax

```rust
// Basic
race\!(a, b, c)

// With biased polling (first listed polled first)
race\! { biased;
    a,
    b,
    c,
}

// Named (for clarity)
race\! {
    cache = check_cache(key),
    db = query_database(key),
}
```

## Biased vs Unbiased

- **Unbiased**: Random polling order (deterministic with seed in lab)
- **Biased**: Left-to-right polling order (useful for fallback patterns)

## Testing

- Basic race of 2 futures
- Verify loser is cancelled
- Verify loser fully drained before return
- Race under parent cancellation
- Race with panicking future
- Biased vs unbiased behavior
- Obligation cleanup in losers

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] race\! macro for 2-16 futures
- [ ] RaceN enum types
- [ ] RaceFutureN implementations
- [ ] Cancel trait
- [ ] Full loser drain (not just drop)
- [ ] Loser outcome inspection
- [ ] Biased/unbiased modes
- [ ] Tests for all scenarios","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-31T21:30:46.355124531Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T08:15:31.384824034Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T08:15:31.384655390Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["combinators","core","race"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-24hd","depends_on_id":"bd-14c0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-31T21:35:13.582287139Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-24hd","depends_on_id":"bd-1sx3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-31T21:30:46.372691372Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2520","title":"Pool integration tests incorrectly use try_acquire expecting resource creation","description":"Tests in tests/pool_tests.rs call try_acquire() expecting it to create new resources, but try_acquire() only returns idle resources. Tests should use acquire() (async) or pre-populate the pool. Affects 11 failing tests.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"IvoryMoose","created_at":"2026-01-26T09:01:31.029002779Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T09:04:41.620395437Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T09:04:41.620326478Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-257b","title":"Obligation tracker: real-time view of held obligations and potential leaks","description":"# Obligation Tracker

## Overview
Real-time dashboard for tracking all obligations (permits, leases, acks) across the
runtime, with leak detection and aging warnings.

## Design

### Obligation Overview
```rust
pub struct ObligationTracker {
    state: Arc<RuntimeState>,
    console: Console,
}

impl ObligationTracker {
    /// List all active obligations.
    pub fn list_obligations(&self) -> Vec<ObligationInfo> {
        self.state.all_obligations()
            .map(|o| ObligationInfo {
                id: o.id(),
                type_name: o.type_name(),
                holder_task: o.holder_task(),
                holder_region: o.holder_region(),
                created_at: o.created_at(),
                age: o.age(),
                state: o.state(),
            })
            .collect()
    }
    
    /// Find potentially leaked obligations.
    pub fn find_potential_leaks(&self, age_threshold: Duration) -> Vec<ObligationInfo> {
        self.list_obligations()
            .into_iter()
            .filter(|o| o.age > age_threshold && o.state.is_active())
            .collect()
    }
    
    /// Get obligations by type.
    pub fn by_type(&self, type_name: &str) -> Vec<ObligationInfo> {
        self.list_obligations()
            .into_iter()
            .filter(|o| o.type_name == type_name)
            .collect()
    }
}
```

### Obligation Types Tracked
- **ConnectionLease**: Database/network connections
- **TransactionLock**: Uncommitted transactions
- **ChannelPermit**: Bounded channel send permits
- **MessageAck**: Unacknowledged queue messages
- **FileLock**: File reservation leases
- **TimerHandle**: Active timer registrations

### Output Format
```
Obligation Tracker
╭─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ Active Obligations: 12  │  Potential Leaks: 2  │  Age > 1m: 5 │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ Type              Count  Oldest    Holder                   │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ ConnectionLease     5    2.3s      Region-2 (HttpHandler)   │
│ TransactionLock     2    45.2s     Task-42 (db_update)      │
│ MessageAck          3    12.1s     Task-17 (process_queue)  │
│ ChannelPermit       2    0.1s      Task-89 (sender)         │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ ⚠️  POTENTIAL LEAKS (age > 60s):                            │
│   TransactionLock held by Task-42 for 45.2s                 │
│     -> Created at: src/db/queries.rs:89                     │
│     -> Recommendation: Check for missing commit/rollback    │
╰─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
```

### Interactive Features (TUI)
- Filter by obligation type
- Sort by age, holder, type
- Click to drill down
- Threshold alerts configuration

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] List all active obligations
- [ ] Group by type
- [ ] Age tracking
- [ ] Leak detection with thresholds
- [ ] Holder information
- [ ] Creation location tracking
- [ ] TUI dashboard
- [ ] Unit tests

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests
- `tracker::tests::list_all_obligations` - Enumerate obligations
- `tracker::tests::filter_by_type` - Type filtering
- `tracker::tests::detect_potential_leak` - Leak detection
- `tracker::tests::age_threshold_filter` - Age-based alerts
- `tracker::tests::holder_info_display` - Show holder details

### Integration Tests
- `tracker::integration::live_obligations` - Real obligations
- `tracker::integration::concurrent_create_release` - Concurrent ops

### Logging Requirements
- TRACE: Individual obligation lookups
- DEBUG: Obligation list queries
- INFO: Potential leak warnings
- WARN: Long-held obligations","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T02:58:03.582168798Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T08:02:03.822599882Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T08:02:03.822527798Z","close_reason":"Implemented ObligationTracker with: list_obligations(), find_potential_leaks(), by_type/by_task/by_region filters, summary(), and render_summary(). Includes ObligationInfo, ObligationStateInfo, TypeSummary, and ObligationSummary types. Added 5 unit tests. Exports added to observability module.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ecosystem-parity","observability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-257b","depends_on_id":"bd-nugw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T02:59:09.223652409Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-257b","depends_on_id":"bd-vpkn","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-01T02:58:52.795213795Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-2581","title":"Supervision trees: policies, strategies, restart backoff","description":"# Supervision Trees

## Goal

Implement supervisor actors that monitor children and apply restart policies on failure.

## Background

Supervision is the Erlang/Akka pattern for fault tolerance: parent actors watch children and decide what to do when they fail. This transforms crashes from disasters into routine events.

## Restart Policies

### OneForOne
- Only the failed child is restarted
- Other children unaffected
- Use when children are independent

### OneForAll
- All children restarted when one fails
- Use when children have shared state dependencies

### RestForOne
- Failed child and all children started after it are restarted
- Use when children have ordered dependencies

### Custom
- User-defined Decide trait implementation
- Full control over restart logic

## SupervisionConfig

```rust
pub struct SupervisionConfig {
    /// Restart policy to apply
    pub policy: RestartPolicy,
    
    /// Max restarts in time window before escalating
    pub max_restarts: u32,
    
    /// Time window for counting restarts
    pub restart_window: Duration,
    
    /// Backoff strategy between restarts
    pub backoff: BackoffStrategy,
    
    /// What to do when max_restarts exceeded
    pub escalation: EscalationPolicy,
}
```

## BackoffStrategy

```rust
pub enum BackoffStrategy {
    /// No delay between restarts
    Immediate,
    
    /// Fixed delay between restarts
    Fixed(Duration),
    
    /// Exponential backoff with jitter
    Exponential {
        initial: Duration,
        max: Duration,
        multiplier: f64,
    },
}
```

## EscalationPolicy

What happens when max_restarts exceeded:
1. **Stop**: Stop the failing actor permanently
2. **Escalate**: Propagate failure to parent supervisor
3. **Restart**: Reset restart counter and try again

## Supervisor Actor

```rust
pub struct Supervisor {
    children: HashMap<ActorId, ChildSpec>,
    config: SupervisionConfig,
    restart_history: VecDeque<Time>,
}

pub struct ChildSpec {
    actor: Box<dyn Actor>,
    config: SupervisionConfig,
    mailbox_config: MailboxConfig,
}
```

## Failure Detection

Supervisor is notified when:
1. Child returns Outcome::Err from receive()
2. Child returns Outcome::Panicked
3. Child stops unexpectedly (not requested by parent)

## Implementation Notes

- Supervisor is itself an actor (supervisors can supervise supervisors)
- Root supervisor created by runtime
- Restart history uses circular buffer for efficiency
- Backoff implemented via Cx::sleep

## Logging & Observability

Every supervision event traced:
- child_failed(actor_id, reason)
- child_restarting(actor_id, attempt, delay)
- child_restarted(actor_id, attempt)
- max_restarts_exceeded(actor_id, escalation)

## Testing

- OneForOne: verify only failed child restarts
- OneForAll: verify all children restart
- RestForOne: verify correct subset restarts
- Backoff: verify delays between restarts
- Max restarts: verify escalation triggers
- Lab runtime: deterministic supervision behavior

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] RestartPolicy enum with 4 policies
- [ ] SupervisionConfig with all options
- [ ] BackoffStrategy with 3 strategies
- [ ] EscalationPolicy with 3 options
- [ ] Supervisor actor implementation
- [ ] Failure detection from child outcomes
- [ ] Restart history tracking
- [ ] Tracing for all supervision events","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-31T21:18:44.425130610Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-31T21:33:46.591423438Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["actors","phase3","supervision"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2581","depends_on_id":"bd-3dcp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-31T21:33:46.591394113Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2581","depends_on_id":"bd-3t9g","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-31T21:18:44.446634911Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"bd-2581","title":"Supervision trees: policies, strategies, restart backoff","description":"# Supervision Trees

## Goal

Implement supervisor actors that monitor children and apply restart policies on failure.

## Background

Supervision is the Erlang/Akka pattern for fault tolerance: parent actors watch children and decide what to do when they fail. This transforms crashes from disasters into routine events.

## Restart Policies

### OneForOne
- Only the failed child is restarted
- Other children unaffected
- Use when children are independent

### OneForAll
- All children restarted when one fails
- Use when children have shared state dependencies

### RestForOne
- Failed child and all children started after it are restarted
- Use when children have ordered dependencies

### Custom
- User-defined Decide trait implementation
- Full control over restart logic

## SupervisionConfig

```rust
pub struct SupervisionConfig {
    /// Restart policy to apply
    pub policy: RestartPolicy,
    
    /// Max restarts in time window before escalating
    pub max_restarts: u32,
    
    /// Time window for counting restarts
    pub restart_window: Duration,
    
    /// Backoff strategy between restarts
    pub backoff: BackoffStrategy,
    
    /// What to do when max_restarts exceeded
    pub escalation: EscalationPolicy,
}
```

## BackoffStrategy

```rust
pub enum BackoffStrategy {
    /// No delay between restarts
    Immediate,
    
    /// Fixed delay between restarts
    Fixed(Duration),
    
    /// Exponential backoff with jitter
    Exponential {
        initial: Duration,
        max: Duration,
        multiplier: f64,
    },
}
```

## EscalationPolicy

What happens when max_restarts exceeded:
1. **Stop**: Stop the failing actor permanently
2. **Escalate**: Propagate failure to parent supervisor
3. **Restart**: Reset restart counter and try again

## Supervisor Actor

```rust
pub struct Supervisor {
    children: HashMap<ActorId, ChildSpec>,
    config: SupervisionConfig,
    restart_history: VecDeque<Time>,
}

pub struct ChildSpec {
    actor: Box<dyn Actor>,
    config: SupervisionConfig,
    mailbox_config: MailboxConfig,
}
```

## Failure Detection

Supervisor is notified when:
1. Child returns Outcome::Err from receive()
2. Child returns Outcome::Panicked
3. Child stops unexpectedly (not requested by parent)

## Implementation Notes

- Supervisor is itself an actor (supervisors can supervise supervisors)
- Root supervisor created by runtime
- Restart history uses circular buffer for efficiency
- Backoff implemented via Cx::sleep

## Logging & Observability

Every supervision event traced:
- child_failed(actor_id, reason)
- child_restarting(actor_id, attempt, delay)
- child_restarted(actor_id, attempt)
- max_restarts_exceeded(actor_id, escalation)

## Testing

- OneForOne: verify only failed child restarts
- OneForAll: verify all children restart
- RestForOne: verify correct subset restarts
- Backoff: verify delays between restarts
- Max restarts: verify escalation triggers
- Lab runtime: deterministic supervision behavior

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] RestartPolicy enum with 4 policies
- [ ] SupervisionConfig with all options
- [ ] BackoffStrategy with 3 strategies
- [ ] EscalationPolicy with 3 options
- [ ] Supervisor actor implementation
- [ ] Failure detection from child outcomes
- [ ] Restart history tracking
- [ ] Tracing for all supervision events","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-31T21:18:44.425130610Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T00:53:18.249521497Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["actors","phase3","supervision"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2581","depends_on_id":"bd-3dcp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-31T21:33:46.591394113Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2581","depends_on_id":"bd-3t9g","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-31T21:18:44.446634911Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
